description_long: This is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular wooden cabinet that shows signs of age, such as scratches and worn edges. The front face features a large, fabric-covered speaker grille above a classic control panel with two round tuning knobs and a horizontal frequency indicator. An extendable metal antenna protrudes from the top center, suggesting the radio is capable of receiving AM/FM signals. The sides and back are plain, finished in the same wood veneer, with visible grain and some scuff marks. The control panel is accented with metallic and plastic elements, and the tuning scale is marked with numbers and lines. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century radios, blending utilitarian function with a warm, retro aesthetic.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with a fabric speaker grille, two tuning knobs, a frequency indicator, and a metal antenna. The cabinet is rectangular and shows signs of wear, giving it a classic, retro look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the speaker grille, two tuning knobs, frequency indicator, and the base of the antenna.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the plain wooden surface and the antenna emerging from the top.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the unadorned wooden back panel with visible grain and wear.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with a plain wooden surface and the antenna visible on top.
